In this video, I'm going to show you how you can obtain your ClickFunnels domain and kind of cover why it's so important that you have one. When you log into ClickFunnels, the first thing you're going to see on your screen is your ClickFunnels dashboard. From here, you want to look in the top right for this little icon that represents your profile picture. If you don't have one, it will be this gray kind of generic body that you've seen on all the websites. If you have one, that will replace it here. You want to go to Account Settings. When Account Settings loads up, you'll see all the options on the left-hand side. This is also where you find your billing and your account history notifications. A lot of important stuff is in here. At the main bottom above Global Unsubscribes is Domains. If you click on Domains, it will take you to a page that most likely will not show any domains here unless you've used ClickFunnels in the past or you've already imported or purchased one. Instead, you'll see the Add New Domain button. When you click this Add New Domain button, it will take you to a page that offers Add Your Own Domain or Register a New Domain. I highly, highly suggest that you just register a new domain as adding your own and importing an existing domain is very risky and it takes a lot more time and it's a lot harder to do. This process changes based on where you purchased the original domain from as well. It's very easy to replace your existing website with your ClickFunnel page, which we do not want to happen. If you have an existing website, you want to leave it alone and have it completely separate from what we're using for your advertisements. That existing website can be used for organic traffic and just growing your name, but we want to have something separate to advertise and gather information with. I really do not suggest going the route of adding your own domain. I would almost always go with registering a new one. Much easier. It's like a three-click process and you're done. We want to go to Choose a Domain. We select this, it will take us to a search option where we can search for all available domains. To cover what a domain is really quickly, to give you a quick rundown of that, if we look at the top of my browser you can see that it says ClickFunnels.com slash Domains. In this example, ClickFunnels.com is the domain and everything that comes after it is a path which we'll learn about later. Domains are so important because we want to have one to be able to host the website and you can host as many funnels as you want with one domain. You almost never really need separate domains unless it's just something you want so that it has a different look or a different name to it. Domains are very important because without it we cannot save a lot of the back-end settings on the funnels. It also is used to verify your website so that when people visit your website it doesn't give them a warning. It lets them know that it's secure and verify that nothing fishy is going on or no fraudulent activity is happening. You always want to verify a domain whenever you're advertising or whenever you have anything connected to a business page actually. Here we're going to search for a domain that's available and give you guys some examples. For example purposes I'm going to search for the domain Jimname and it will let us know every single domain that has Jimname in it. From here we can see Jimname.net, Jimname.org, etc. When searching for a domain you can pretty much make it whatever you want. The only suggestions I have is that you don't make it something like Jimname 6 week challenge or 42 AD cocks or something like that. The reason for that is because if you have 6 week challenge inside of your domain and you end up switching your play later on or using a different funnel it doesn't really match up. If you're doing a Lean by Halloween or a little black dress challenge and the link says Jimname 6 week challenge or 42 AD cocks it will cause some confusion with your leads and the less confusion the better and it just doesn't look very good. I'll try to keep it simple. Another thing you can do is you can add the state that you're in if it happens to be taken so let's say just Jimname is taken all of them are gone and you can't use it. You can add the state name at the end. You can change it up a little bit and kind of make it your own. In this example here we're going to grab a domain really quickly. We can see that Jimname.net is available. Jimname.com is taken. That's okay. I'll use Jimname.net. On the right hand side you'll see a $20 button. When you click this $20 button it will take you to a page that will let you know if it's available or not which it should be since it allows you to get this far. It will tell you everything it came with as well as a $20 price point and allow you to kind of register it and get it going. If you hit this pay $20 it will register the domain and you'll be able to grab it. From there you'll be able to set it up in the settings on any funnels you have.
